segments.en: But first, his early career ... as I've said, he was a writer, he was at the height of his career in Paris and he was very highly regarded by his colleagues.
segments.zh: 但首先，他的早期职业生涯……正如我所说，他是一位作家，在巴黎达到了事业巅峰，深受同行尊敬。就文学而言，他是浪漫主义运动的领军人物。然而，除了文学天才的身份，他还就社会贫困等问题发表了许多演讲。他对此以及教育等其他需要变革的领域感受强烈。这种直言不讳的批评并不受法国统治者欢迎，最终，皇帝拿破仑三世命令维克多·雨果离开巴黎不得返回，也就是说，将他流放了。

segments.en: As far as literature was concerned, he was the leading figure of the Romantic movement.
However, as well as being a literary genius, he also gave many speeches about issues like the level of poverty in his society.
He felt very strongly about this and about other areas where change was needed, like education.

This kind of outspoken criticism was not well liked by the rulers of France and, eventually, the emperor – Napoleon III – told Victor Hugo to leave Paris and not return; in other words, he sent him into exile.
So Victor Hugo was forced to reside in other parts of Europe.
segments.zh: 所以维克多·雨果被迫居住在欧洲其他地方。根西岛实际上是他第三个流放地，他于1855年抵达那里。在根西岛期间他创作了大量作品——包括《悲惨世界》——为此他需要花大量时间待在那里的家中。这处房产是他用之前在法国出版诗集所得的钱购买的。这是他唯一拥有过的房产，他对此非常自豪。

segments.en: Guernsey was actually his third place of exile and he landed there in 1855.
He produced a lot while on Guernsey – including Les Misérables – and to do this, he had to spend a great deal of time in the home that he had there.
This was a property that he bought using the money he'd made in France from the publication of a collection of his poetry.
It was the only property he ever owned, and he was very proud of it.

The property Victor Hugo bought on Guernsey was a large, five-storey house in the capital town of St Peter Port and he lived there for 15 years, returning to France in 1870 when Napoleon's Empire collapsed.
segments.zh:
维克多·雨果在根西岛购买的房产是一栋位于首府圣彼得港的五层大房子，他在那里住了15年，直到1870年拿破仑帝国垮台后才返回法国。他装饰和布置了每一层，方式独特而美妙，许多人认为房子内部是一件“艺术品”。如今它是一座博物馆，每年吸引20万游客。

他和家人住在这栋房子里……家人的肖像至今仍挂在一楼的房间里，还有他在旅途中画的他认为很重要的画作。在其他一楼房间里，有他设计并喜爱的巨大挂毯。墙壁上覆盖着深色木镶板，这是维克多·雨果用他在市场上购买的木质家具自己制作的。这些物品相对便宜，他用它们制作了复杂的雕刻。它们给底层营造了一种阴暗而相当肃穆的氛围。

segments.en: He decorated and furnished each level, or floor, of the house in unique and wonderful ways, and many people consider the inside of the house to be a 'work of art'.
Today it's a museum that attracts 200,000 visitors a year.
He lived in the house with his family ... and portraits of its members still hang in rooms on the ground floor, along with drawings that he did during his travels that he felt were important to him.
In other ground-floor rooms, there are huge tapestries that he would have designed and loved.
The walls are covered in dark wood panelling that Victor Hugo created himself using wooden furniture that he bought in the market.
The items were relatively inexpensive, and he used them to create intricate carvings.
They gave an atmosphere to the lower level that was shadowy and rather solemn.
On the next level of the house there are two impressive lounges, where he entertained his guests.
segments.zh: 在房子的下一层有两个令人印象深刻的客厅，他在这里招待客人。一个客厅全是红色装饰，比如沙发和墙布，另一个是蓝色。这些区域有很强的中国风影响，比如墙纸图案和灯具——这些灯具是他通过仿制原版自己制作的。

segments.en: One lounge has entirely red furnishings, such as sofas and wall coverings, and the other blue.
There's a strong Chinese influence in these areas in things like the wallpaper pattern and the lamps – which he would have made himself by copying original versions.
His library, where he left many of his favourite books, forms the hallway to the third floor and was a comfortable area where he could relax and enjoy his afternoons.
segments.zh: 他的书房里留有许多他最喜欢的书，构成了通往三楼的走廊，是一个舒适的区域，他可以在那里放松并享受下午时光。然后，在房子的最顶层，有一个叫“瞭望台”的房间——之所以这么叫是因为它可以俯瞰港口。与相对阴暗的底层形成对比，这里光线充足，就像一个玻璃办公室，他经常在那里写作到午餐时间——通常是在他的书桌前。

segments.en: And then, at the very top of the house, there's a room called the Lookout – called because it looks out over the harbour.
In contrast to the rather dark lower levels, it's full of light and was like a glass office where he would write until lunchtime – often at his desk.
So, Victor Hugo was a man of many talents, but he was also true to his values.
segments.zh:
所以，维克多·雨果是一个多才多艺的人，但他也忠于自己的价值观。在根西岛的房子里居住时，他招待过许多其他著名作家，但他还每周邀请一大群来自岛上贫困地区的当地孩子来吃晚餐。更有甚者，他亲自为他们上菜，这在当时是一种非同寻常的姿态。

1927年，这栋房子由他的亲属拥有，他们决定将其捐赠给巴黎市。此后，房子根据当时的照片进行了修复，正如我之前提到的，现在是一座对公众开放的博物馆。

segments.en: While living in his house on Guernsey, he entertained many other famous writers, but he also invited a large group of local children from the deprived areas of the island to dinner once a week.
What's more, he served them their food, which was an extraordinary gesture for the time period.
In 1927, the house was owned by his relatives, and they decided to donate it to the city of Paris.
It has since been restored using photographs from the period and, as I mentioned earlier, is now a museum that is open to the public.

items.prompt: We know more about its overall ... than about its author.
He spoke publicly about social issues, such as ... and education.

pairs.note: spoke publicly 替换为 gave many speeches
such as 替换为 like
原词重现

paraphrase.explain: 题干中的'spoke publicly about social issues'对应原文的'gave many speeches about issues'，'such as'对应'like'，'education'原词出现，从而定位到答案'poverty'。

items.prompt: Victor Hugo had to live elsewhere in ...

pairs.note: live elsewhere 和 reside in other parts 都表示'住在别处'，是同义替换。

paraphrase.explain: 题干说 Victor Hugo 不得不住在别处，定位句说 forced to reside in other parts of Europe，其中 'reside in other parts' 替换了 'live elsewhere'，从而定位到答案 Europe。

items.prompt: He used his income from the sale of some ... he had written to buy a house on Guernsey.

pairs.q: income from the sale of some ... he had written
pairs.p: money he'd made in France from the publication of a collection of his poetry
pairs.note: 题干说'卖了他写的...的收入'，原文说'在法国出版他的诗集赚的钱'，用'出版诗集'替换了'卖作品'。
题干用'some ... he had written'模糊指代，原文明确说是'他的诗集'，这是同义替换。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到他买房子用的钱来自出版诗集，与题干'用卖作品收入买房'对应，所以答案填poetry。

items.prompt: The ground floor contains portraits, ... and tapestries that he valued.

pairs.note: 题干和原文完全相同的词，直接定位。
题干和原文完全相同的词，直接定位。
题干中未出现，但原文中与portraits并列，是答案词。

paraphrase.explain: 题干提到ground floor有portraits和tapestries，原文中ground floor部分提到portraits和drawings，以及tapestries，因此答案drawings出现在portraits之后。

items.prompt: He bought cheap ... made of wood and turned this into beautiful wall carvings.

pairs.note: cheap 和 inexpensive 都表示'便宜的'，是同义替换。
made of wood 和 wooden 都表示'木制的'，是同义替换。
题干中的 furniture 在原文中直接出现，是原词复现。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到 Victor Hugo 用他在市场上买的木制家具（wooden furniture）制作了精美的雕刻，其中 inexpensive 对应 cheap，wooden 对应 made of wood，furniture 原词复现，因此答案应为 furniture。

items.prompt: The first floor consists of furnished areas with wallpaper and ... that have a Chinese design.

pairs.note: furnished areas 与 furnishings 同义，都指家具陈设区域
原文直接出现 wallpaper，与题干一致
答案词 lamps 在原文中直接出现

paraphrase.explain: 题干问一楼有哪些带中国设计的区域，定位句提到 'the next level' 即一楼，并列举了 wallpaper pattern 和 lamps，其中 lamps 是答案。

items.prompt: He wrote in a room at the top of the house that had a view of the ...

pairs.q: room at the top of the house
pairs.p: at the very top of the house, there's a room
pairs.note: 原文用'at the very top'替换了题干中的'at the top'，并明确说'有一个房间'。
'looks out over'意思是'俯瞰'，与'有……的视野'同义。
原文直接出现'harbour'，与答案一致。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'at the very top of the house, there's a room called the Lookout – called because it looks out over the harbour'，其中'at the very top'对应题干'at the top'，'looks out over'对应'had a view of'，且直接出现'harbour'，因此答案就是harbour。

items.prompt: He entertained other writers as well as poor ... in his house.

pairs.note: poor 被替换为 deprived areas，都表示贫困地区

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到他邀请当地贫困儿童每周来吃饭，对应题干中的 entertained poor children in his house，因此答案 children 来自 local children。

items.prompt: Victor Hugo's ... gave ownership of the house to the city of Paris in 1927.

pairs.note: donate 意为捐赠，与 gave ownership 同义
his relatives 对应题干中 Victor Hugo's 后缺失的名词

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到 'the house was owned by his relatives, and they decided to donate it to the city of Paris'，其中 his relatives 对应题干中的 Victor Hugo's ...，donate 对应 gave ownership。
